title: Palace of the Woolamander

{{Quote|There are supposedly an extraordinary amount of woolamanders that live there. It seems that when the temple was first found, instead of finding the builders of the structure, the locals found a den thriving with the creatures. Stories go on to say that the place is rich in resources, thus allowing these creatures to breed at alarming rates.|a commoner, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:ac">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=herald|a commoner|Yavin 4}}</ref>}}
The '''Palace of the Woolamander''', or '''Woolamander Palace''', was an ancient [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] temple, southeast of the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]], in the [[Val'Arnos Jungle]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].

==History==
{{Quote|The Woolamander Palace can be found on Yavin IV. I do not know the history of the place. We have received several reports that affiliates of the Alliance still operate from its ruins from time to time. It can be found in the central area of Yavin IV.|Colonel Darkstone speaking about the Palace of the Woolamander|<ref name="SWG:CD" />}}
The Palace of the Woolamander was built by [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] slaves controlled by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] who needed more Sith structures to augment his power,<ref name="PotJS" /> in [[3997 BBY]].<ref name="TEC" /> He also kept the [[Golden Globe]]—a globular device which imprisoned the souls of the children of the Massassi—in a sealed chamber.<ref name="The Golden Globe">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: The Golden Globe]]''</ref>

During the last centuries of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the [[Kushiban]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ikrit]] came to the Palace and discovered the Golden Globe. Knowing that he was unable to free the trapped souls, he placed himself in a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] trance to wait the arrival of those who would break the curse.<ref name="The Golden Globe" />

[[File:Woolamander_statue.jpg|thumb|250px|An ancient statue of a Woolamander]]
By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it had been reduced to a crumbling ruin and was discovered by the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Naturalist/Legends|naturalist]] [[Dr'uun Unnh/Legends|Dr'uun Unnh]]. He gave the temple its name because he found a pack of [[Woolamander/Legends|woolamanders]] in the temple during his exploration. These creatures raised a loud, howling alarm when he entered and chased him away by pelting the Sullustan. Due to this and the fact that it was crumbling and falling apart, D'ruun Unnh deemed that the palace held little interest.<ref name="TISWU">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> a year after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], there were reported sightings of various members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], or at the very least affiliates of the group, operating from within the ruins of the palace from time to time, acting as one of the few Rebel bases on the moon since the Rebels were [[Evacuation of Yavin|driven out]] by the Empire, which acted as the reason why it was singled out as one of three possible locations of a prisoner transfer log that [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darkstone]] dispatched an Imperial unit to retrieve in an attempt to rescue [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Bledsoe]], the others being the [[Mokk Stronghold]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] and the [[Droid Engineer's Cave|Droid Engineer cave]] on [[Lok/Legends|Lok]].<ref name="SWG:CD" />

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Yavin IV was the headquarters of the [[Senate Planetary Intelligence Network]], as well as the temporary seat of the [[New Republic Provisional Council]].<ref name="GDV" />

In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] trainees [[Anakin Solo]] and [[Tahiri Veila]] discovered the Palace of the Woolamander during a trek of the jungle surrounding the [[Jedi Praxeum]]. They discovered the Golden Globe and Ikrit though they were also unable to break it at first.<ref name="The Golden Globe" /> Later, during a trip to nearby [[Yavin 8/Legends|Yavin 8]], the two Jedi discovered ancient carvings within a deep cave indicating its origins.<ref name="JJK:LW">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Lyric's World]]''</ref> However, before they could do this, the pair had to undergo a test in the deserts of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Having survived, Anakin and Tahiri returned to the Palace of the Woolamander and broke the curse, freeing countless trapped Massassi.<ref name="JJK:P">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Promises]]''</ref>

During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]] students took shelter in a cave beneath the Palace of the Woolamander during the [[Peace Brigade]] [[Invasion of Yavin 4|attack]] on the [[Jedi Praxeum]].<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|III|6|Palace of the Woolamander}}</ref>
